title: Trichocentrum ultrajectinum
what_description: Trichocentrum ultrajectinum is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid with a stout stem, 10-20 cm in height, and 2-3 linear, lanceolate leaves, 10-20 cm long. The inflorescence is a raceme, 10-20 cm long, bearing 2-3 flowers, each 3-4 cm in diameter, with a greenish-yellow sepals and petals, and a yellow labellum with a yellow, horn-like callus. The plant is found in the tropical forests of Central and South America, and blooms in the spring. Trichocentrum ultrajectinum has been classified as an endangered species due to its limited distribution and habitat destruction.

The health benefits of Trichocentrum ultrajectinum include its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as combat bacterial and fungal infections.
